title: job.published payload (v1)
description: A job posting was published — the google-indexing worker re-indexes the public listing (Indexing API URL_UPDATED). HONEST STATUS: this is a CONSUMER-ONLY contract. The worker (apps/job-service/src/workers/google-indexing.worker.ts) subscribes to tenant.*.job.published on a JOB_EVENTS stream, but NO producer publishes it today and JOB_EVENTS is NOT in CORE_STREAMS — the worker explicitly logs "idle - publisher slice not deployed" and stays quiet when the stream is absent. This schema documents the MINIMAL shape the consumer reads (it only needs a job-posting id off the payload).
x-producer: (none active — consumer-only contract; publisher slice not deployed)
x-consumers: job-service google-indexing worker (enqueue Google Indexing API URL_UPDATED for the listing)

tenantId.description: Owning tenant (mandatory; also on the envelope).

jobPostingId.description: The published JobPosting id. The worker reads this off the payload (readJobPostingId) to resolve the public URL; without it the event is ignored.
